refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on minimizing energy per ton moved aligns with the acceptance criteria and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and verify that energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.
sub_frs: Minimize energy consumption during vehicle operation
Optimize task scheduling to reduce idle and unnecessary movements
Monitor and report energy efficiency metrics

candidate_dps: Real-time energy monitoring and reporting system
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of energy-efficient vehicle powertrain design, intelligent task scheduling algorithm, and real-time energy monitoring and reporting system

potential_sources: Energy monitoring data dependency on vehicle sensors
Task scheduling algorithm requiring telemetry data
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear interfaces; dependencies are manageable with known interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate energy and task telemetry data
Risk that task scheduling optimization may conflict with safety policies
Risk that vehicle powertrain improvements may increase complexity or cost
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure task scheduling DP explicitly incorporates safety constraints to mitigate risk.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Implements energy-efficient vehicle powertrain design and integrates energy sensors for monitoring

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time energy consumption and operational data to Teleoperation and Safety Services modules

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data exchange for task scheduling and monitoring

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of powertrain and energy sensors; low diffusion as energy data is centralized.
Contains task scheduling algorithm; moderate diffusion due to interaction with Vehicle and Safety Services.
Monitors energy efficiency for safety compliance; higher diffusion due to cross-module alerts.
System-level coordination; high diffusion as it integrates all modules.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency physical components and sensors are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize diffusion and centralize ownership.
containment_risks: Potential for duplicated energy data processing in Teleoperation and Safety Services if interfaces are not well defined
Risk of interface overload on Telemetry and Network Link if data volume is high
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear data interface contracts and consider data aggregation to reduce diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well defined and the selected DPs logically satisfy the FRs with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Energy data may be redundantly processed across modules
Task scheduling algorithm diffusion across Teleoperation and Vehicle
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for physical energy efficiency DPs; software DPs are split but interfaces are known.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring energy consumption and tonnage via Telemetry interface and validating against E_target.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of physical energy efficiency to Vehicle and software functions to Teleoperation and Safety Services.

recommended_revisions: Incorporate safety constraints explicitly in task scheduling DP
Refine interface definitions to reduce data diffusion
Plan verification activities to include cross-module data validation

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, risks related to safety integration and data diffusion require addressing before proceeding.
next_level_sub_frs: Minimize energy consumption during vehicle operation
Optimize task scheduling to reduce idle and unnecessary movements
Monitor and report energy efficiency metrics

revise.why: To explicitly incorporate safety constraints into the task scheduling algorithm and refine data handling to reduce diffusion.

assumptions: Accurate telemetry data is available and reliable
Safety policies can be integrated into task scheduling without major performance loss
open_risks: Potential conflicts between energy efficiency optimization and safety requirements
Data diffusion causing interface overload or duplicated processing
evidence_needed: Demonstration of task scheduling algorithm respecting safety constraints
Data flow analysis showing minimal diffusion and clear ownership
